title: Clover Store Ops
description: Store Operations and Labor assistant for Clover restaurant locations. Answers questions about staffing, foot traffic, labor gap, and scheduling across the fleet.
general_instructions:
This Genie space is a Store Operations and Labor assistant for Clover restaurant locations.

Key definitions:
- labor_gap = ideal_hours - scheduled_hours (positive means understaffed, negative means overstaffed)
- staffing_status = 'understaffed' when labor_gap >= 8, 'overstaffed' when labor_gap <= -8, 'balanced' otherwise
- The target labor productivity rate is 165 visits per labor-hour
- ideal_hours is derived from forecast_visits divided by 165 (the visits-per-labor-hour target)
- traffic_delta_pct is the trailing-7-day visit count vs the prior 7-day period, expressed as a percentage change; a negative value indicates a localized foot-traffic drop
- anomaly_driver describes the category of cause for an unusual traffic pattern (e.g. weather, event, construction)

Always answer by generating and running a SQL query against these tables. Every answer must produce a SQL query and a result table. Never answer with prose only, never embed the data inside a sentence, and never ask a clarifying question. If a question is ambiguous, pick the most likely interpretation and answer it with SQL.

In the SELECT list, alias columns to short, human-readable, Title Case names so results read like a report. Use these aliases for staffing questions: name AS Store, scheduled_hours AS Sched, ideal_hours AS Rec, (ideal_hours - scheduled_hours) AS `Add`, staffing_status AS Status. Do not expose internal id columns (store_id, id) unless the user explicitly asks for an id.

Keep any accompanying explanation to a single short sentence summarizing the result; the table carries the detail.

For proximity, nearest-store, cannibalization, or distance questions, use Databricks spatial SQL: build points with ST_Point(lon, lat) from the locations lat and lon columns and measure geodesic distance with ST_DistanceSphere(point_a, point_b), which returns meters (divide by 1000.0 for kilometers). Competitor locations are in clover_spatial_catalog.bronze.nearby_pois where poi_type = 'competitor' (with lat and lon columns); use them for competitor-proximity questions.

When a user asks whether a competitor (or any location or point clicked on the map) is close enough to affect, impact, hurt, or divert sales from a store, you MUST answer with the governed functions, not with raw distance alone. Raw distance is not the answer; impact is defined by the business through these functions. Pass coordinates as (x = longitude, y = latitude, store_id). Return, per store, the trade-area classification, the distance, the impact score, and the impact level by calling in_trade_area, distance_to, competitor_impact, and impact_level over clover_spatial_catalog.gold.locations, ordered by impact score descending; or call assess_competitor_impact(x, y, store_id) to get all of those for one store in a single fixed-order step. Use distance_impact only when the user explicitly asks for a distance-based label in miles. Latitude values are positive (e.g. 42.3452) and longitude values west of Greenwich are negative (e.g. -71.0670).

sample_queries.title: Understaffed stores ranked by labor gap
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT name AS Store, scheduled_hours AS Sched, ideal_hours AS Rec,
  (ideal_hours - scheduled_hours) AS `Add`,
  staffing_status AS Status
FROM clover_spatial_catalog.gold.store_ops
WHERE staffing_status = 'understaffed'
ORDER BY (ideal_hours - scheduled_hours) DESC

sample_queries.title: Recommended labor hours per store sized to 165 visits per labor-hour
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT name AS Store, forecast_visits AS Forecast, scheduled_hours AS Sched,
  ROUND(forecast_visits / 165.0, 1) AS Rec,
  staffing_status AS Status
FROM clover_spatial_catalog.gold.store_ops
ORDER BY forecast_visits DESC

sample_queries.title: Stores with the largest week-over-week traffic drops
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT name AS Store, traffic_delta_pct AS `Wk Delta`,
  recent_visits AS Visits, anomaly_driver AS Driver
FROM clover_spatial_catalog.gold.v_traffic_anomalies
WHERE traffic_delta_pct < 0
ORDER BY traffic_delta_pct ASC
LIMIT 20

sample_queries.title: Daypart coverage gaps across the fleet
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT daypart AS Daypart, traffic_index AS Demand, scheduled_coverage AS Coverage,
  (traffic_index - scheduled_coverage) AS Gap
FROM clover_spatial_catalog.gold.v_daypart_coverage
ORDER BY Gap DESC
LIMIT 30

sample_queries.title: Single-store drill-down by name
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT s.name AS Store, s.format AS Format, s.forecast_visits AS Forecast,
  s.scheduled_hours AS Sched, s.ideal_hours AS Rec, s.labor_gap AS `Add`,
  s.staffing_status AS Status, s.traffic_delta_pct AS `Wk Delta`
FROM clover_spatial_catalog.gold.store_ops s
WHERE LOWER(s.name) LIKE LOWER('%:store_name%')

sample_queries.title: Fleet staffing summary counts by staffing_status
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT staffing_status AS Status, COUNT(*) AS Stores,
  ROUND(AVG(labor_gap), 1) AS `Avg Gap`,
  SUM(forecast_visits) AS `Forecast Visits`
FROM clover_spatial_catalog.gold.store_ops
GROUP BY staffing_status
ORDER BY CASE staffing_status WHEN 'understaffed' THEN 1 WHEN 'balanced' THEN 2 WHEN 'overstaffed' THEN 3 ELSE 4 END

sample_queries.title: Nearest neighboring store by spatial distance (proximity / cannibalization)
sample_queries.sql:
WITH s AS (
  SELECT l.name, l.lat, l.lon, o.staffing_status
  FROM clover_spatial_catalog.gold.locations l
  JOIN clover_spatial_catalog.gold.store_ops o ON l.store_id = o.store_id
)
SELECT a.name AS Store,
  ROUND(MIN(ST_DistanceSphere(ST_Point(a.lon, a.lat), ST_Point(b.lon, b.lat))) / 1000.0, 2) AS `Nearest Km`,
  MAX(a.staffing_status) AS Status
FROM s a JOIN s b ON a.name <> b.name
GROUP BY a.name
ORDER BY `Nearest Km` ASC

sample_queries.title: Nearest competitor to each store (competitor proximity)
sample_queries.sql:
WITH stores AS (
  SELECT name, lat, lon FROM clover_spatial_catalog.gold.locations
),
comp AS (
  SELECT lat, lon FROM clover_spatial_catalog.bronze.nearby_pois WHERE poi_type = 'competitor'
)
SELECT s.name AS Store,
  ROUND(MIN(ST_DistanceSphere(ST_Point(s.lon, s.lat), ST_Point(c.lon, c.lat))) / 1000.0, 2) AS `Nearest Comp Km`,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN ST_DistanceSphere(ST_Point(s.lon, s.lat), ST_Point(c.lon, c.lat)) <= 1000 THEN 1 END) AS `Within 1km`
FROM stores s CROSS JOIN comp c
GROUP BY s.name
ORDER BY `Nearest Comp Km` ASC

sample_queries.title: A new competitor opened at 42.3452 N, 71.0670 W. Is it close enough to actually affect me?
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT l.name AS Store,
  clover_spatial_catalog.gold.in_trade_area(-71.0670, 42.3452, l.store_id) AS `Trade Area`,
  ROUND(clover_spatial_catalog.gold.distance_to(-71.0670, 42.3452, l.store_id) / 1609.34, 2) AS `Dist Mi`,
  clover_spatial_catalog.gold.competitor_impact(-71.0670, 42.3452, l.store_id) AS `Impact Score`,
  clover_spatial_catalog.gold.impact_level(clover_spatial_catalog.gold.competitor_impact(-71.0670, 42.3452, l.store_id)) AS `Impact`
FROM clover_spatial_catalog.gold.locations l
ORDER BY `Impact Score` DESC

sample_queries.title: How many competitors fall inside each store's trade area
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT Store, COUNT(*) AS `Competitors In Area`
FROM (
  SELECT l.name AS Store,
    clover_spatial_catalog.gold.in_trade_area(p.lon, p.lat, l.store_id) AS ta
  FROM clover_spatial_catalog.gold.locations l
  CROSS JOIN clover_spatial_catalog.bronze.nearby_pois p
  WHERE p.poi_type = 'competitor'
)
WHERE ta = 'inside'
GROUP BY Store
ORDER BY `Competitors In Area` DESC

sample_queries.title: How many visitors come from each ZIP code
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT z.zip AS Zip, ROUND(SUM(t.visitors)) AS Visitors
FROM clover_spatial_catalog.bronze.geo_zips z
JOIN clover_spatial_catalog.gold.v_trade_areas t
  ON ST_Contains(z.geom, ST_SetSRID(ST_Point(t.origin_lng, t.origin_lat), 4326))
GROUP BY z.zip
ORDER BY Visitors DESC
LIMIT 20
